Description: Device has lost external power, and is runinng on internal battery
Probable Causes:
a). External supply has failed
b). Internal power supply has failed
c). Power cable has become dislodged
Proposed Action:
a). Check if the electric supply is powered off
b). Check the power cable
c). Check if the equipment will power up, and replace if necessary

Probable Causes: Battery voltage is below acceptable level
Proposed Action:
a). Check if the standby battery is installed in the device
b). Disable the alarm if there is no battery
c). Replace the battery

Probable Causes: The device has been rotated to it's side
Proposed Action: a). Check installation of the equipment

Description: Internal temperature of the Device exceeds safe working threshold.
Probable Causes:
a). Installation of equipment does not allow for adequate air circulation
b). Fan unit for this equipment has failed.
c). Ambient air temperature within the equipment room has elevated beyond working range.
Proposed Action:
a). Check the device temperature and alarm threshold in OMT. The default threshold is 85oC
b). Check if the separation of between adjacent AUs is too close without fan
c). Check if there is no ventilation or air condition for cooling temperature

Description: A device that was previously detected is no longer responding
Probable Causes:
a). Equipment has been removed from the system
b). The optical fibre is disconnected, or an optical transceiver has failed
c). The power to this device is off
Proposed Action:
a). If equipment has been removed from service, delete the equipment from the topology
b). Check the fibre path and restore optical connection
c). Check power to the device
d). Replace the equipment

Probable Causes: Equipment fault or unknown disruption
Proposed Action:
a). Perform a hardware reboot of the equipment
b). Replace equipment and return for repair

Probable Causes: Equipment fault or unknown disruption
Proposed Action:
a). Perform a hardware reboot of the equipment
b). Replace equipment and return for repair

Probable Causes: Equipment fault or unknown disruption
Proposed Action:
a). Perform a hardware reboot of the equipment
b). Replace equipment and return for repair

Proposed Action:

b). Replace equipment and return for repair

Probable Causes: PA Module 1 temperature is too high
Proposed Action:
a). Adjust module 1 downlink attenuation
b). Decrease module 1 output power

Probable Causes: PA Module 2 temperature is too high
Proposed Action:
a). Adjust module 2 downlink attenuation
b). Decrease module 2 output power

Probable Causes: PA Module 3 temperature is too high
Proposed Action:
a). Adjust module 3 downlink attenuation
b). Decrease module 3 output power

Probable Causes: PA Module 4 temperature is too high
Proposed Action:
a). Adjust module 4 downlink attenuation
b). Decrease module 4 output power

Description: Optical link has failed to initiate/maintain connection with the end device
Probable Causes:
a). SFP not installed, or removed
b). SFP has failed
c). Fibre cable is disconnected
d). Optical modules of two sides do not pair
Proposed Action:
a). Disable this alarm for all unused optical ports
b). Verify if SFP is plugged in and connected. Replace if failed.
c). Check if the fiber cable is damaged or connector is dirty
d). Check if the SFP modules at both ends are a mated pair

Alarm Name: Radio Module1 DL Output Over-power Alarm
Description: Remote Unit DL output power exceeds the user-assigned threshold
Probable Causes:
a). The DL input signal exceeds system design parameters
b). The system gain settings have been changed
Proposed Action:
a). Compare expected DL output power to the alarm threshold.
b). Verify the input power from all BTS has not changed
c). Verify attenuation setting in AU and RU have not changed
d). Adjust attenuator settings as necessary

Alarm Name: Radio Module1 DL Output Under-power Alarm
Description: Remote Unit DL output power is lower than the user-assigned threshold
Probable Causes:
a). The DL input signal to AU is lower than expected, perhaps because a BTS has been turned off or removed
b). The DL PA has failed
c). The RF Switch in AU-AC module is turned off
d). The AU-AC module mapping in AU is incorrect
Proposed Action:
a). Compare expected DL output power to the alarm threshold.
b). Verify the input power from all BTS has not changed
c). Verify attenuation setting in AU and RU have not changed
d). Adjust attenuator settings as necessary

Probable Causes: External device, system or environment alarm has triggered
Proposed Action:
a). Verify that the External alarm input should be activated
b). Check the status of the external device

Probable Causes: The cover has been removed or the internal sensor is broken
Proposed Action:
a). Check if the device case is open or warranty seal is broken
b). Disable the alarm or replace equipment

Probable Causes:
a). Feed cable issue (wet/dirt inside/bent/damaged)
b). RF connector is loose
c). Antenna issue
d). Wrong connection to filter port
Proposed Action:
a). Check the VSWR value and alarm threshold. The default threshold is 2.5
b). Check if the RU output port is open-load or connector is loose (use correct torque wrench)
c). Check RU port connection to combiner is incorrect band
d). Check the feed cable and antenna

Probable Causes:
a). Feed cable issue (wet/dirt inside/bent/damaged)
b). RF connector is loose
c). Antenna issue
d). Wrong connection to filter port
Proposed Action:
a). Check the VSWR value and alarm threshold. The default threshold is 2.5
b). Check if the RU output port is open-load or connector is loose (use correct torque wrench)
c). Check RU port connection to combiner is incorrect band
d). Check the feed cable and antenna

Alarm Name: Radio Module1 ALC Over Range Alarm
Description: Downlink ALC range (AU-AC) or Uplink ALC range (RU) has been exceeded. For DL ALC the AU-AC RF Switch is set to OFF. Manual reset is required.
Probable Causes: DL input power at AU, or Uplink input power at RU, is too high and there is no more attenuation left for ALC.
Proposed Action:
a). Check if the composite DL power in AU-AC (after attenuation) exceeds 0 dBm
b). Check uplink signal for over-power condition or local interference

Alarm Name: Radio Module1 Port1 DL Input Under-power Alarm
Description: AU-AC Module 1 Port1-4 input power under the rated threshold
Probable Causes:
a). AU-AC input power is under the user-defined threshold
b). There is no input power
c). External attenuator value is too high
Proposed Action:
a). Check DL input power and the alarm threshold.
b). Check the input power from BTS if possible
c). Use proper attenuator before AU input port
d). Configure proper attenuation in AU

Description: The fiber transmission port of a daisy-changed device has toggled from OP-s to OP-m
Probable Causes: The primary fibre path to the device has become disconnetced, and the redundant loopback path has assumed control
Proposed Action:
a). Check integrity of the primary optical fibre cable
b). Check if the SFP module in primary optical path has failed
c). Clean optical connectors

Description: Band Configuration of AU-AC is incorrect, or cummulative bandwidth exceeds the maximum
Probable Causes: Band Configuration is incorrect during commissioning
Proposed Action:
a). Check the Band Configuration in AU
b). Verify the AU-AC module is the correct band
c). Configure proper bandwidth and DL/UL center frequency
d). Check if the system transmission bandwidth is over the maxmimum value

Description: OP-transceiver (SFP) failure
Probable Causes:
a). SFP not installed, or removed
b). SFP has failed
c). Fibre cable is disconnected
d). Optical modules of two sides do not pair
Proposed Action:
a). Disable this alarm for uniused optical ports
b). Verify if SFP is plugged in and connected. Replace if failed.
c). Check if the fiber cable is broken or connector is dirty
d). Check if the SFP modules of two sides are a mated pair

Description: Master AU fails to detect heartbeat from NMS/LMS. This alarm is displayed in OMT only, and will not generate an SNMP trap
Probable Causes:
a). Networking connection issue
b). LMS/NMS is down
c). LMS/NMS is removed from system
Proposed Action:
a). Ping the LMS server IP address to see if the server is alive
b). Check if the LMS/NMS is down or removed from system
c). Check the connection of AU and server

Description: NMS/LMS fails to detect heartbeat from Primary AU
Probable Causes:
a). Networking connection issue
b). Heartbeat of AU is not configured
c). AU is down
d). AU is removed from system
Proposed Action:
a). Use the server computer to ping AU IP address to see if the AU is alive
b). Check the heartbeat of AU
c). Check if the AU is down or removed from system
d). Check the connection of AU and server

Description:
Internal temperature of the Device exceeds safe working threshold.
Or the fan1 isn't working normally.
Probable Causes:
a). Installation of equipment does not allow for adequate air circulation
b). Fan1 unit for this equipment has failed.
c). Ambient air temperature within the equipment room has elevated beyond working range.
Proposed Action:
a). Check the device temperature and threshold in OMT. The default threshold is 95oC
b). Check if the separation of between adjacent AUs is too close without fan
c). Check if there is no ventilation or air condition for cooling temperature

Proposed Action:

b). Check if the separation of between adjacent AUs is too close without fan
c). Check if there is no ventilation or air condition for cooling temperature
